title: Troubleshooting, Security, Recovery, and Operations Handoff · Linux Stakeholder Mission
summary: A new technician must independently operate and troubleshoot a Linux workstation. Resolve a changed multi-layer incident and publish a least-privilege, recovery-tested handoff. Planned artifact: Deliver Hardened Workstation Operations Portfolio with acceptance evidence, rubric defense, reflection, and a changed-constraint extension. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Test local process, local socket, route, name resolution, transport, and application protocol with the smallest useful probe.
Separate server host identity, user authentication, authorization, agent forwarding, and known-host decisions.
Define script inputs, outputs, side effects, preconditions, exit codes, and rerun behavior before implementation.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# linux-basics cumulative operations evidence notebook
# Commands run only in the deterministic stateful terminal simulator.
# Record hypothesis, command, expected signal, observation, conclusion, and recovery.

steps.why: Retrieving Operations Handoff through the concrete a complete Linux operations evidence portfolio case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Write an accessible runbook with prerequisites, exact observations, safe actions, expected signals, exceptions, recovery, and ownership.

hints: Name the Operations Handoff precondition or invariant that the project case must preserve.

Trace this concrete case before choosing: A project Linux incident crosses filesystem, permission, process, service, and network evidence. Preserve a baseline, change one bounded layer, record exit status, and verify rollback on case 2. Competency probe: Write an accessible runbook with prerequisites, exact observations, safe actions, expected signals, exceptions, recovery, and ownership.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: A list of commands is a sufficient operations handoff.
